questions.question: Modest
questions.solution:
• Meaning: Modest = विनम्र / साधारण
  • Opposite Idea: Conceited = आत्ममुग्ध / घमंडी
  • Example: A conceited person is not modest.
  • Exam Tip: Humble ↔ Proud is a common antonym pair.

questions.question: Raze
questions.solution:
• Meaning: Raze = नष्ट करना / ध्वस्त करना
  • Opposite Idea: Build = निर्माण करना
  • Example: They built a new house instead of razing it.
  • Exam Tip: Destroy ↔ Construct.

questions.question: ROUGH
questions.solution:
• Meaning: Rough = खुरदरा / असमान
  • Opposite Idea: Smooth = चिकना / सहज
  • Example: The surface was smooth, not rough.
  • Exam Tip: Uneven ↔ Even surfaces.

questions.question: Tenacious
questions.solution:
• Meaning: Tenacious = जिद्दी / दृढ़
  • Opposite Idea: Yielding = समर्पण करने वाला / लचीला
  • Example: A yielding person is not tenacious.
  • Exam Tip: Strong-willed ↔ Flexible or submissive.

questions.question: Criticise
questions.solution:
• Meaning: Criticise = आलोचना करना
  • Opposite Idea: Praise = सराहना करना
  • Example: Instead of criticising, she praised the work.
  • Exam Tip: Condemn ↔ Commend.

questions.question: Camaraderie
questions.solution:
• Meaning: Camaraderie = मित्रता / दोस्ताना माहौल
  • Opposite Idea: Dislike = नापसंद / दुश्मनी
  • Example: Dislike breaks camaraderie.
  • Exam Tip: Friendship ↔ Enmity.

questions.question: Rely
questions.solution:
• Meaning: Rely = भरोसा करना
  • Opposite Idea: Distrust = अविश्वास करना
  • Example: Distrust means you do not rely on someone.
  • Exam Tip: Trust ↔ Doubt.

questions.question: Appal
questions.solution:
• Meaning: Appal = भयभीत / स्तब्ध करना
  • Opposite Idea: Assure = आश्वस्त करना
  • Example: He assured them instead of appalling them.
  • Exam Tip: Shock ↔ Comfort.

questions.question: Prey
questions.solution:
• Meaning: Prey = शिकार
  • Opposite Idea: Predator = शिकारी
  • Example: Predator hunts, prey is hunted.
  • Exam Tip: Victim ↔ Hunter.

questions.question: Optional
questions.solution:
• Meaning: Optional = ऐच्छिक / इच्छानुसार
  • Opposite Idea: Compulsory = अनिवार्य
  • Example: Attendance is compulsory, not optional.
  • Exam Tip: Voluntary ↔ Mandatory.

questions.question: Linger
questions.solution:
• Meaning: Linger = देर तक ठहरना
  • Opposite Idea: Leave = छोड़ देना / चले जाना
  • Example: She decided to leave immediately, not linger.
  • Exam Tip: Stay ↔ Go.

questions.question: Ascent
questions.solution:
• Meaning: Ascent = आरोहण / चढ़ाई
  • Opposite Idea: Descent = अवरोहण / उतार
  • Example: The descent was steep.
  • Exam Tip: Up ↔ Down.

questions.question: CLENCH
questions.solution:
• Meaning: Clench = कसना
  • Opposite Idea: Relax = ढीला करना
  • Example: He relaxed his fist after clenching it.
  • Exam Tip: Tight ↔ Loose.
